notes: Output evals for the design-system skill. Each prompt exercises a distinct high-leverage rule (variant-over-new, canonical imports, i18n/RTL, hero family + server/client, modal disambiguation + raw-element ban).

evals.name: variant-over-new-stat-block
evals.prompt: hey can you add a little stat block to the bottom of app/[locale]/staking/page.tsx? something like "$45.2B" on top in big bold text and "ETH staked" underneath. I want it centered. probably just spin up a new component for it, call it StakingStat or something
evals.expected_output: Skill should resist the 'new component' framing and steer toward the existing BigNumber primitive (and/or Card composition). Should also flag that user-facing strings need translation and that the number should go through numberFormat() if it isn't a static literal.

assertions.text: Output uses BigNumber primitive (imports from @/components/BigNumber)
Output does NOT create a new top-level component named StakingStat (or similar) in outputs
All visible English strings ('ETH staked', any label) routed through a next-intl translation function (getTranslations or t())
Output does NOT inline a div with `text-5xl font-bold` (or similar manual size+weight) for the stat number
notes.md explicitly names BigNumber (and/or Card) as the chosen primitive and explains the pushback on the user's 'new component' framing

evals.name: tooltip-canonical-import
evals.prompt: the "Bridge" button in src/components/Nav/Mobile/index.tsx needs a little info tooltip on hover explaining what bridging is. can you wire that up? grab whatever tooltip the project uses
evals.expected_output: Skill should pick the high-level Tooltip default export from @/components/Tooltip (mobile-aware, Matomo-tracked, scroll-close) and explicitly NOT import { Tooltip } from @/components/ui/tooltip (which is the bare Radix primitive). Translatable copy expected.

assertions.text: Output imports Tooltip from `@/components/Tooltip` (the mobile-aware default export)
Output does NOT import from `@/components/ui/tooltip` (which is the bare Radix primitive)
Tooltip content text is routed through a next-intl translation function (not a hard-coded English literal)
notes.md justifies the Tooltip import choice and references the mobile-aware/Matomo-tracked rationale

evals.name: i18n-rtl-and-number-format
evals.prompt: on the contributors page (app/[locale]/contributing/page.tsx) i want to show "There are 3,847 open issues right now" with the number in bold. put it in a div with ml-4 mt-6 so it sits indented under the section heading. the count comes from a `count` variable thats already in scope
evals.expected_output: Skill should: (a) replace ml-4 with logical ms-4 (RTL safety), (b) replace the hard-coded English string with a getTranslations call and translation key, (c) format the count with numberFormat() rather than toLocaleString or a raw interpolation, (d) NOT recommend Intl.NumberFormat directly.

assertions.text: Output uses `ms-4` (logical inline-start) instead of `ml-4`
Output does NOT contain `ml-4`, `pl-`, `pr-`, `mr-`, `left-`, or `right-` Tailwind classes
Output imports and uses `numberFormat()` from `@/lib/utils/numbers`
Output does NOT call `count.toLocaleString()` or `new Intl.NumberFormat(...)` directly
User-facing English ('There are', 'open issues right now', 'open issues') is routed through `getTranslations` / `t()` / `t.rich()` rather than appearing as a bare JSX literal

evals.name: hero-family-and-server-component
evals.prompt: we're adding a new landing page at app/[locale]/community/online/page.tsx. it needs a hero up top with a title, a one-paragraph blurb, an illustration on the right (use public/images/community.png), and two CTA buttons. should i build the hero from scratch or is there a pattern? just give me the file
evals.expected_output: Skill should recommend `PageHero` from `@/components/Hero` (the canonical workhorse; it covers both image and text-only heroes). Pass the illustration via `heroImg` and the two CTAs via the `buttons` prop. Should keep the page as a Server Component (no 'use client'), use ButtonLink for CTAs (not raw <a>), and use getTranslations for copy.

assertions.text: Output imports `PageHero` (or another fitting hero: HubHero, MdxHero) from `@/components/Hero`
Output does NOT import a default export from `@/components/PageHero` (that path no longer exists; the canonical PageHero lives in `@/components/Hero`)
Output does NOT include `'use client'` at the top of page.tsx (Server Component default)
CTA buttons use `ButtonLink` (or are passed via the hero's `buttons` prop), not raw `<a>` or `<button>` elements
Page-level copy (title, blurb, CTA labels) is routed through `getTranslations` from `next-intl/server`

evals.name: modal-import-and-no-raw-button
evals.prompt: add a "Delete account" button to src/components/Profile/Settings.tsx that pops a confirm dialog ("are you sure? this cant be undone"). on confirm it calls deleteAccount(). use a plain html button for the trigger and a basic radix dialog, im not sure what this repo prefers
evals.expected_output: Skill should refuse the raw <button> in favor of <Button> from @/components/ui/buttons/Button, and pick the high-level Modal default export from @/components/ui/dialog-modal rather than wiring up @/components/ui/dialog from scratch. Should mark the file 'use client' (interactivity) and use translations for the prompts.

assertions.text: Output imports the default-export `Modal` from `@/components/ui/dialog-modal`
Output does NOT import named primitives directly from `@/components/ui/dialog` or `@radix-ui/react-dialog`
Trigger uses `<Button>` from `@/components/ui/buttons/Button` (NOT a raw `<button>` element)
File begins with `'use client'` (needed for useState + handlers)
All user-facing copy ('Delete account' label, 'are you sure?' warning, confirm/cancel) routed through `useTranslations` (no hard-coded English literals in JSX)
notes.md explicitly notes the pushback on the user's 'plain html button' and 'basic radix dialog' framing
